Our Kenya AA coffee has less of an acidic "bite" than other African coffees, allowing those who prefer lower levels of coffee acidity to experience the dynamic wine-like brilliance, fragrant fruit notes, and silky body of our Kenya AA coffee.

catalog_content:
Item Name: Korintje Cinnamon Chips, Cassia Bark - 1 oz.
Bullet Point 1: These chips of sweet cassia bark cinnamon possess a perfect amount of bark and bite.
Bullet Point 2: Cassia bark cinnamon chips are spicy-sweet, rolled, woody pieces of Korintje cinnamon perfect for infusing curry, dessert sauces or a hot drink
Bullet Point 3: All Natural, Gluten-Free, Kosher Parve, Non-GMO
Product Description: Cassia is simply the bark of a laurel tree, a tropical evergreen. Nearly the same as cinnamon, it is sometimes called Chinese Cinnamon. The two spices can be used interchangeably. Cassia is a bit coarser and the taste is not quite as delicate. In all appearances, cassia looks and nearly smells the same as cinnamon. Not quite as sweet as cinnamon, it is used more in savory dishes in China. Cassia bark is perfect for use when making homemade chai tea blends, pickling spice blends, or if you’re brewing your own spirits. In addition, we love to keep these cassia chips on hand for the spice grinder to add to coffee and tea, or to toss into curries or masalas. During winter, take a small spoonful and drop them into simmering pots of cider and wine you plan to mull through the afternoon before a big holiday party. Since we use Korintje cinnamon you get a rounded, sweet cinnamon flavor with a mild bite that won’t overpower your recipe. Use cassia spice in the same manner as Cinnamon.
Value: 1.0
Unit: Ounce
overall_summary: Natural, gluten-free, and non-GMO cassia bark chips positioned as a versatile spice for culinary and beverage applications. Mid-tier pricing likely due to specialty spice status and multi-use functionality. Packaging and brand tier not explicitly indicated.
